title: Neotheater
notes: This breakout album has some good proof that AJR can make good songs. Their iconic, sample-heavy sound is great for casual music listeners, and the band has a lot of energy. However, I think they maybe relied too much on samples and production rather than musicianship, which I think they have plenty of (evidenced by Dear Winter). The writing could use some work in my opinion, there is a lot of sudden shifts that maybe could have more in between them such as the intro to both 'Next Up Forever' and 'Finale'
highlights: I like that AJR are visionary and experimental. I think they have unique topics, and a VERY unique sound. And when they get it right, its really catchy and fun.
lowlights: Unfortunately, especially on this album, there are songs that they don't get right. The sound heavily relies on production and clips rather than fully live music, which I am not a fan of. I think it narrows what they can do and what they can sound like.
